mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer spending power, which forces equity valuations down over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and construction activity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60d returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks and reduced corporate output, triggering short-term negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >70% of months where INDPRO declines by more than 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces anticipatory monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates labor force participation shifts that mask underlying economic weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a Rank IC below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly correlates with aggregate corporate earnings expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 20d returns with a t-statistic greater than 1.5 in quarterly regression models.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly drop in housing starts signals a collapse in residential investment and related consumer durables spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months where housing starts fall by more than 5%.
